mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately suppress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and yields positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or returns are negative in > 60% of co-occurring months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger immediate negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression, which boosts equity risk premiums and generates positive forward returns as investors rotate into growth assets.
failure_condition: The combination of rate cuts and falling inflation fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced consumer spending capacity, which systematically depresses corporate earnings expectations and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or the rolling 2-year rank correlation remains below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand that exceeds baseline expectations, prompting immediate positive equity repricing as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 3.0%, or the signal shows no predictive alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising employment during a supply-constrained regime indicates bottlenecks and inflationary pressure, which forces monetary tightening expectations and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-specific predictive power compared to a baseline model.
